summary: Frontend-only review of ED Finder v3.32 in the Emergent preview env. Verified the WebGL black hole shader, the new map overlay checkboxes (jump-range + timeline), and the wiring of /api/map/timeline. F4–F7 (rerank, confidence pill, rationale banner, share button) could NOT be exercised because the backend is dead in this preview env (502 on every /api call) — these features only render after a successful search response. Backend cannot be started here without major env work (see action items).

critical.issue: Supervisor runs `uvicorn server:app` but the app module is `main.py` (no server.py). Backend has been crash-looping with 'Could not import module "server"' since startup. /var/log/supervisor/backend.err.log is full of this error.

Backend requires asyncpg, redis[asyncio], pydantic-settings, slowapi, prometheus-fastapi-instrumentator (per requirements.txt). NONE are installed in /root/.venv. `import asyncpg` fails immediately at /app/backend/main.py line 60.
Backend is PostgreSQL/asyncpg + Redis based (per docker-compose.yml). Neither Postgres nor Redis is running in this container. Only mongodb is up via supervisor — but the app does not use MongoDB.
All API endpoints return 502 from the preview URL. Verified: /api/health=502, /api/map/regions=502, /api/map/timeline=502 (timeline auto-fired by the timeline checkbox toggle).
Could not be tested. Backend down.

integration_issues.issue: Could not test — search yields zero results (backend 502), so the results toolbar (with the ⚖️ Tune Weights button) never renders. The button only exists in the DOM after a successful search.
Could not test — same root cause. No cards rendered without backend data. Confidence pill, rationale banner, and Share button all live inside a card.

Timeline checkbox correctly fires GET /api/map/timeline (verified in console: '[timeline] fetch failed Error: HTTP 502'). #map-time-bars stays empty because backend 502. The wiring is correct; the failure is purely backend availability.

issues: frontend supervisor program runs `yarn start` in /app/frontend, but /app/frontend has no package.json — it is a static HTML/JS app (index.html, app.js, style.css, sw.js). yarn errors with 'Couldn't find a package.json file in /app/frontend' on every retry. Frontend supervisor program is in FATAL state. (I worked around by running `python3 -m http.server 3000` so the preview URL serves the static index.html.)

action_items: URGENT: Reconcile environment. The /app codebase (PostgreSQL+asyncpg backend, static HTML frontend) does NOT match the Emergent supervisor template (FastAPI:server:app + React/yarn). Either (a) update supervisor (it is marked READONLY) so backend uses `main:app` and frontend uses a static server, or (b) repackage the app to fit the template.
Install backend deps: `pip install -r /app/backend/requirements.txt` into /root/.venv (asyncpg, redis[asyncio], pydantic-settings, slowapi, prometheus-fastapi-instrumentator, etc.).
Provision PostgreSQL 16 + Redis (or stub them out) so /app/backend/main.py can connect on startup. Without them the lifespan handler will fail.
Once backend is up, re-test F3, F4, F5, F6, F7, F10. All UI is in place and wired (testids match spec); only backend availability is blocking validation.
Provide /app/frontend/.env with REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL (file is missing) — although for this static app the frontend uses same-origin /api/* fetches, so this is informational only.
Note: the `/api/health` endpoint, when backend is up, will be hit through the kubernetes ingress at https://d97182e8-ce46-4bcf-9c15-f3c39188d9fd.preview.emergentagent.com/api/health.
critical_code_review_comments: main.py is 2009 lines — exceeds the 700-line guideline. Split into routers (search.py, map.py, ratings.py, watchlist.py, system_detail.py).

index.html is 12,754 lines with inline GLSL + inline JS. This single-file approach makes review/maintenance painful. Recommend extracting WebGL BH module, weights panel, map overlays into separate JS modules (already have app.js — use it).

Frontend index.html has 12k+ inline JS with no source maps; any runtime error stack trace points to bare line numbers in index.html (e.g., 'at _mapFetchTimeline (...:12357:24)'), making debugging hard.
Supervisor config marked READONLY but it does not match the codebase — this is an env/template mismatch that should be flagged to the platform team.
F9 timeline fetch logs 'HTTP 502' as a warning but the user-facing UI gives no feedback that data could not load — consider showing a small inline error in #map-time-bars.

Result-card-dependent features (weights button, confidence pill, rationale, share btn) are entirely gated on backend success. Consider a dev-mode mock or a small seed so the static UI can be QAed offline.

success_rate.frontend: F1 PASS, F2 PASS, F8 PASS (testids present, controls in DOM), F9 PASS (testids present, fetch wiring correct), F3/F4/F5/F6/F7 BLOCKED on backend (~40% verified)

context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend MUST be brought up before F3-F7 and F10 can be retested. The supervisor file is read-only and points uvicorn at `server:app`; this codebase has `main:app`. /root/.venv is missing asyncpg/redis. PostgreSQL+Redis not running. Workaround applied to get the static frontend serving on port 3000: I started `python3 -m http.server 3000 --bind 0.0.0.0` in /app/frontend (a background nohup). The preview URL https://d97182e8-ce46-4bcf-9c15-f3c39188d9fd.preview.emergentagent.com/ now serves the index.html via that fallback. To re-test, click the sidebar '🌌 Map' button (text-based, no testid), THEN the testids `map-overlay-jumprange-cb` and `map-overlay-timeline-cb` become interactable. For F3-F7, you need a working /api/local/search or /api/search/galaxy first. Also: when calling page.check() on these checkboxes Playwright reports 'state did not change' (because the onchange handlers fire side-effects but Playwright's check verification is timing-sensitive); use el.click() via JS evaluate instead, as I did.
rca of the issue: ROOT CAUSE: Environment template mismatch. The Emergent preview env was provisioned with the standard template (supervisor expects FastAPI server:app + Mongo + React/yarn), but the actual /app codebase is ED Finder v3.x — a PostgreSQL/asyncpg/Redis backend + a static HTML frontend. Result: backend program fails to import (`server` module missing, `asyncpg` not installed), frontend program fails to start (`yarn start` with no package.json). All /api/* requests therefore return 502 via the kube ingress. The new v3.1 features (BH shader, weights panel, jump-range, timeline) are correctly implemented in the source — the BH WebGL shader rendered flawlessly with no compile/link errors — but anything dependent on the backend cannot be exercised. MITIGATION: see action_items above. Workaround applied: static http.server on port 3000 lets the preview serve index.html so UI-only assertions could be made.
